\title{Sparse Innovation Regression with Compressed Sensing (SIR-CS)\\Draft: Method and Synthetic Experiments}
\author{}
\date{}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
We summarize a hybrid estimator that predicts a global component with machine learning and recovers a sparse innovation by compressed sensing under linear measurements.
We then document a reproducible \emph{Phase~0} synthetic baseline: ten independent seeds, measurement ratios up to $\rho=0.6$, a refined $\ell_1$ penalty grid for validation-based model selection, and standard diagnostics (curves versus $\rho$, gains over ml-only, parity plots, residual histograms, and example traces).
A subsequent \emph{Step~1} section compares FISTA versus SPGL1 on the same protocol.
Real-data evaluation is outlined as future work.
\end{abstract}
